constraints.coverage_assumption: The active deep_comparison task on the active_task (how paper 2 builds on paper 1) is paused; the user has shifted to a new actionable request: choose which of the two selected surveys to read first for designing an agent evaluation harness. The harness is assumed to target general LLM-based agent evaluation unless the user later specifies a multi-turn conversational-only scope.

entities: recommended reading order for designing an agent evaluation harness
justification grounded in scope and frameworks
normalized_question: Recommend which of the two selected surveys on LLM-based agent evaluation the user should read first when designing an agent evaluation harness, grounded in the selected evidence.

obligations.description: Identify what aspects of paper 1 (8b9e2f64d565137ca85ae75029e77fff) are directly relevant to designing an agent evaluation harness.
Identify what aspects of paper 2 (726cfbbd5683cdfabbf4c24e2bf8f0b2) are directly relevant to designing an agent evaluation harness.

Clarify that the recommendation assumes the user's agent evaluation harness targets general/multi-application agents rather than only multi-turn conversational agents; both choices should be presented as contingent on that scope.

Provide a reason grounded in evidence for why one paper is recommended first over the other, addressing coverage breadth, taxonomy/methodology depth, and applicability to harness architecture decisions.

raw_question: Which one should I read first if I am designing an agent evaluation harness?
